location.office_time_required: 3-4 days a week in one of our SF Bay Area offices

secondary_archetype: Technical PM (LLM/Model Hub platform, API and 3rd-party partnerships)

level_signals: 4+ years of experience in product management
Compensation range $160K-$240K base, consistent with mid-to-senior IC at a well-funded AI startup
Scope involves owning LLM roadmap, cost projections, and capacity planning independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4+ years of experience in product management at SaaS companies, primarily serving large enterprises with responsibilities spanning AI/ML Ops and external-facing platform capabilities.
must_haves.qualifier: at SaaS companies, primarily serving large enterprises with AI/ML Ops and external-facing platform responsibilities

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have a strong technical background and experience with both UX and API driven products and 3rd party product partnerships.
You are an excellent written and verbal communicator.
You have a proven track record of taking ownership, taking initiative, and delivering results.
You collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners.
You have a learning and growth mindset.
You are mission-first and understand that your success is measured by your product and team's success.
You are an early adopter in building with or adopting AI for your own product craft and you are excited about helping customers accelerate their own AI adoption journey.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Having built or worked with LLMs for at-scale products is a major plus.

scope.surface: Glean Model Hub — LLM portfolio, model evaluation, inference provider relationships, customer LLM experimentation, usage/cost/capacity planning
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer upgrade adoption rates for latest LLMs
Customer enablement and AI adoption journey progress
core_responsibilities: Evaluate LLM models and define the roadmap for growing Glean's LLM portfolio
Manage relationships with inference and model providers
Determine product strategy for allowing customers to experiment with different LLMs
Own projections of LLM usage, cost, and capacity planning
Drive customer enablement and upgrade adoption journey for the latest LLMs and LLM-native capabilities
Spending a lot of time with customers to deeply understand their knowledge needs
Developing key parts of the product roadmap, marrying customers' needs with product vision
Empowering the team by giving context, setting direction, and building alignment
Coordinating cross-functionally with leadership, go-to-market teams, and other key stakeholders
Building processes that will scale as the team and company go through rapid growth

compensation.equity_form: equity eligible (mentioned alongside variable compensation and benefits)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and wide ($160K-$240K base), competitive for enterprise AI
Clear product surface and ownership (Glean Model Hub, not a vague AI role)
High-visibility role at a recognized fast-growing AI company (Fast Company Top 10, Forbes AI 50, etc.)
Benefits package well-described including home office stipend, education and wellness stipends
red_flags: Role title is 'AI Quality' but the JD body describes a 'PM for Glean Model Hub' — notable mismatch between title and described responsibilities
Hybrid requirement of 3-4 days/week in SF Bay Area is close to full-time onsite, restricting candidate pool significantly
Visa sponsorship not mentioned; unclear for international candidates
Wide compensation range ($80K spread) makes level and offer expectations opaque

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement + comp range)

scope.business_model (inferred from company description and product surface)
location.work_auth (stated as open based on DEI statement and no citizenship requirement mentioned)

ambiguities: Role title is 'Product Manager, AI Quality' but the JD body consistently describes the role as 'PM for Glean Model Hub' with no mention of 'AI Quality' as a product area. Possible title mismatch or placeholder title.

Most requirements are stated as unconditional bullets under 'About you' without explicit 'Required' or 'Preferred' labels; classified as hard must_haves based on unconditional phrasing, except the explicitly flagged 'major plus' item.
